bid_packages.pdf_name: 26-007 RFCSP Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation (1).pdf
bid_packages.summary: This document is a solicitation notice for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project at Angelo State University. It includes complete demolition and replacement of interior finishes, mechanical, plumbing, electrical systems, and new IT infrastructure.

bid_packages.pdf_name: 26-007 RFCSP Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation (2).pdf
bid_packages.summary: This document is a solicitation notice for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project at Angelo State University. It includes complete demolition and replacement of interior finishes, mechanical, plumbing, electrical systems, and new IT infrastructure.

bid_packages.pdf_name: 26-007 RFCSP Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation.pdf
bid_packages.summary: This document is a solicitation notice for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project at Angelo State University. It includes complete demolition and replacement of interior finishes, mechanical, plumbing, electrical systems, and new IT infrastructure.

[other] This document is a Department of Labor Wage Determination, which outlines the prevailing wage rates for various labor categories in a specific geographic area. It is a standard requirement for federally funded construction projects to ensure compliance with labor laws. The document does not contain specific project details.

bid_packages.project_name: San Marcos Aquatic Resource Center Shop & 2 Stall Garage Building

unit_prices.description: Remove and replace the three overhead sectional doors to include hardware, weather stripping and openers.
Remove and replace existing light fixtures with LED light fixtures to meet OSHA requirements.
Remove all suspended ceiling grids and tiles.
Remove and replace the existing three exterior personnel doors with insulated hollow metal doors to include frames, hardware, weather stripping and threshold.
Terminate, cap and remove existing gas lines from the building.
Remove and replace existing gutter system to include and replace down spouts.
Remove and replace existing roof with new roofing sheets.
Remove existing gas fired unit heaters.

bid_packages.summary: This bid schedule requests pricing for various construction tasks including the removal and replacement of doors, light fixtures, ceiling grids, gas lines, gutter systems, roofing sheets, and unit heaters at the San Marcos Aquatic Resource Center Shop & 2 Stall Garage Building.

bid_packages.project_location: Cleburne High School, 850 N. Nolan River Road, Cleburne, Texas 76033
bid_packages.summary: Cleburne Independent School District is accepting competitive sealed proposals for the renovation of cafeteria serving lines at Cleburne High School. Proposals are due by 2:00 PM on June 8, 2026.

bid_packages.project_name: Alter Command Post and Nondestructive Inspection (NDI) Room
bid_packages.project_location: Naval Air Station Joint Reserve Base, Fort Worth, TX
inclusions: Furnish all labor, materials, equipment, supervision, and services necessary to renovate the designated spaces into complete, durable, and fully operational facilities.
Perform all required interior wall demolition and construction, painting, caulking, carpentry, and associated electrical, mechanical, and plumbing work to deliver the finished spaces.

Ensure all work complies strictly with applicable federal, state, and local codes, as well as all DoD standards, including the Unified Facilities Criteria (UFCs) and Unified Facilities Guide Specifications (UFGSs) cited in any project documentation.
Provide any and all components, services, or materials required to deliver a complete and fully functional system, even if not explicitly specified in this SOW.
Protect all adjacent vehicles, equipment, landscaping, and facilities from overspray, debris, and damage.
Install and maintain a clearly marked construction boundary fence to identify the limits of the work area.
Comply with all applicable Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), U.S. Army Corps of Engineers EM 385‑1‑1, and State safety requirements.
Maintain a clean job site at all times. Demolition debris and excess materials shall be removed daily.
Coordinate inspections with the Contracting Officer’s Representative (COR) prior to commencing and during critical phases of work.
Request a Pre-Final Inspection at approximately 75% completion to identify deficiencies requiring correction prior to final completion.
Request a Final Acceptance Inspection at 100% completion, submitted at least 14 days in advance with written assurance that all deficiencies have been corrected.
Provide proof of insurance.
Submit electronic submittals on the AF 3000 Material Approval Submittal form for each item listed on the AF 66 Submittal Register, as applicable.
Provide a submittal register, updated at least twice per month.
Provide bi-weekly meeting minutes for each progress meeting, updated schedule by task, planned work for the next week.
Provide a Letter of Guarantee that warrants the project against defects in materials and workmanship for one year from the date of final acceptance.
Provide a minimum one (1) year warranty on all workmanship and standard manufacturer’s warranty on products.
Electrical power and water shall be provided as required. The Contractor is responsible for any required connections.
Obtain and maintain all necessary base access for its personnel, including subcontractors.
bid_packages.summary: This document outlines the scope of work for the renovation of the Command Post and Nondestructive Inspection (NDI) Room at the 136th Airlift Wing, Naval Air Station Joint Reserve Base, Fort Worth, TX. The project involves turnkey renovation of designated spaces, ensuring compliance with federal, state, and local codes, and DoD standards. The contractor is responsible for all labor, materials, and services necessary to deliver fully operational facilities.

[general instructions to bidders] This document provides detailed construction instructions for alterations involving metal stud framing, gypsum board installation, painting, and flooring to match existing finishes. It includes specifications for installing doors, electrical outlets, lighting, and HVAC modifications. The document also outlines security measures for doors and mantrap areas, as well as requirements for emergency systems and communication infrastructure. Key facts: Project Number: DDPM262001; Install VCT: AZrock, Shale Gray #V-866; Install new carpet: Shaw Contract, Clear Tile Style# 59564, color Moonstone 62500; Paint standard color: Sherwin-Williams SW1016 Silver Screen

[amendment of solicitation] This document is an amendment to solicitation W50S7626QA001, specifically incorporating revision 1 to the B1672 (CLIN 0001) plans. It includes modifications to contract clauses, particularly regarding contract drawings and specifications. The amendment outlines responsibilities for the contractor in handling and verifying drawings and specifications. Key facts: Project Number: DDPM262101; Location: Fort Worth, TX Key dates: 03 May 2026; 13 May 2026

bid_packages.project_location: College Station, TX, Harrington Education Center Office Tower, 0435
bid_packages.summary: Notice of Project for the Harrington Education Center Lab 303 Renovation at Texas A&M University. Includes project details and submission information for the CSP RFP & HSP.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880767322_26-007 Carr EFA Dressing Room Renocation RFCSP.pdf
bid_packages.summary: The document is a Request for Competitive Sealed Proposals for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project at Angelo State University. The project involves complete demolition and replacement of interior finishes, mechanical, plumbing, and electrical systems, along with new IT infrastructure and ADA code elements. The bid is due on June 5, 2026.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880829538_26-007 Carr EFA Dressing Room Renocation Attachment A.pdf

unit_prices.description: Include a cash/contingency allowance for unforeseen scope of work.

bid_packages.summary: The bid form requests proposals for construction services for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project. It includes a base proposal amount and a specified allowance for unforeseen work. The form outlines certifications and affirmations required from the respondent.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880857161_Attachment B.2 - Sample Construction Services Agreement.pdf
bid_packages.summary: [sample construction services agreement] This document is a sample construction services agreement for projects at Angelo State University. It outlines the scope of work, contract sum, time of commencement and completion, and HUB subcontracting commitments. It also includes provisions for liquidated damages, dispute resolution, indemnity, and various legal and procedural requirements. Key facts: HUB contracting commitment: 30% of the total contract sum; Liquidated damages: $###.00 per calendar day

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880869361_Attachment C - 2010 Uniform General Conditions and Supplementary General Conditions dated (TTUS edited 09.07.2023).pdf
bid_packages.summary: [general instructions to bidders] This document outlines the Uniform General Conditions and Supplementary General Conditions for construction contracts under the Texas Tech University System. It incorporates specific supplementary conditions applicable to all Texas Tech System projects, highlighting material changes from the Texas Facilities Commission's 2010 edition. The document includes detailed articles on definitions, wage rates, responsibilities, subcontracting plans, bonds, insurance, safety, quality control, schedules, payments, changes, completion, warranties, termination, and dispute resolution. Key facts: Document revision date: 09/07/2023; Applicable to all Texas Tech System and member institution construction projects

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880887164_Attachment D - HSP (Historically Underutilized Businesses (HUB) Subcontracting Plan) documents.pdf
bid_packages.summary: This document is a HUB Subcontracting Plan (HSP) required by the State of Texas for contracts with probable subcontracting opportunities. It outlines the requirements for respondents to identify and engage Texas certified HUB vendors for subcontracting work, and includes sections for respondent information, subcontracting intentions, and good faith effort methods. The plan aims to promote equal business opportunities for economically disadvantaged persons.

[other] This document is a Texas Sales and Use Tax Exemption Certification form. It is used by purchasers to claim exemption from sales and use taxes for certain taxable items. The form includes fields for the purchaser's name, address, and the reason for claiming the exemption. It also contains a disclaimer about the legal implications of misuse.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880917414_Attachment F.1 - Tom Green County Prevailing Wage 2023 (TTUS).pdf
bid_packages.summary: [other] This document is a prevailing wage determination for a construction project at Angelo State University in Tom Green County. It lists the prevailing wage rates for various trade classifications as of July 2023. Contractors must classify workers according to these rates and ensure they are paid accordingly. Key facts: Construction type: Building; Location: Tom Green County; Date: July 2023

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1779308778671_26-007 Carr EFA Dressing Room Addendum #1.pdf
bid_packages.summary: This document is an addendum to the Request for Competitive Sealed Proposals for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project at Angelo State University. It includes project information, schedule, proposal requirements, and evaluation criteria.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_518571_1779300513068_RFCSP 2026-0608-01 CHS Cafeteria Serving Line Renovation.pdf
bid_packages.project_location: Cleburne High School, 850 N. Nolan River Road, Cleburne, Texas 76033
inclusions: Complete removal and disposal of existing serving lines.
Procurement and delivery of all new equipment.
Installation of the renovated serving line system.
Ensure cafeteria is fully operational by the start of the 2026-2027 school year.
Comply with all National, State and Local codes and pay all applicable costs, fees and permits.
Prices must include all transportation costs.
bid_packages.summary: The document is a trade bid package for the renovation of cafeteria serving lines at Cleburne High School. It includes the removal and disposal of existing equipment, procurement and installation of new equipment, and ensuring the cafeteria is operational by the 2026-2027 school year. The project is managed by Cleburne ISD with a budget of $295,000.

[other] This document is a Department of Labor Wage Determination, which outlines the prevailing wage rates for various labor categories in a specific geographic area. It is typically used to ensure compliance with wage standards on federally funded construction projects. The document is largely boilerplate and does not contain specific project details.

The project involves complete demolition and replacement of interior finishes, mechanical, plumbing, and electrical systems, along with new IT infrastructure and ADA code elements. bid_packages.summary: The bid form requests proposals for construction services for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project. The form also outlines liquidated damages for late completion and requires HUB contracting commitments.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880857161_Attachment B.2 - Sample Construction Services Agreement.pdf
bid_packages.summary: [other] This document is a sample construction services agreement for Angelo State University, part of the Texas Tech University System. It outlines the terms and conditions under which a contractor will perform construction work, including scope, contract sum, time of completion, and HUB subcontracting commitments. The agreement also covers liquidated damages, dispute resolution, indemnity, and various legal and procedural provisions.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880869361_Attachment C - 2010 Uniform General Conditions and Supplementary General Conditions dated (TTUS edited 09.07.2023).pdf
bid_packages.summary: [general instructions to bidders] This document contains the Texas Tech University System's Uniform General Conditions and Supplementary General Conditions for construction contracts. It outlines the responsibilities of the owner and contractor, wage rates, insurance requirements, construction safety, quality control, and dispute resolution procedures. The document is primarily boilerplate, providing standard terms and conditions applicable to all Texas Tech System construction projects.

bid_packages.pdf_name: ESBD_516718_1778880887164_Attachment D - HSP (Historically Underutilized Businesses (HUB) Subcontracting Plan) documents.pdf
bid_packages.summary: This document is a HUB Subcontracting Plan (HSP) required by the State of Texas for contracts with probable subcontracting opportunities. It outlines the requirements for respondents to identify and engage Texas certified HUB vendors, and includes sections for subcontracting intentions, self-performing justification, and good faith effort methods. The document does not include any priceable line items or alternates.

[other] This document is a Texas Sales and Use Tax Exemption Certification form. It allows a purchaser to claim an exemption from sales and use taxes for certain taxable items. aggregated_inclusions.text: Furnish all labor, materials, equipment, supervision, and services necessary to renovate the designated spaces into complete, durable, and fully operational facilities.
Perform all required interior wall demolition and construction, painting, caulking, carpentry, and associated electrical, mechanical, and plumbing work to deliver the finished spaces.

Ensure all work complies strictly with applicable federal, state, and local codes, as well as all DoD standards, including the Unified Facilities Criteria (UFCs) and Unified Facilities Guide Specifications (UFGSs) cited in any project documentation.
Provide any and all components, services, or materials required to deliver a complete and fully functional system, even if not explicitly specified in this SOW.
Protect all adjacent vehicles, equipment, landscaping, and facilities from overspray, debris, and damage.
Install and maintain a clearly marked construction boundary fence to identify the limits of the work area.
Comply with all applicable Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), U.S. Army Corps of Engineers EM 385‑1‑1, and State safety requirements.
Maintain a clean job site at all times. Demolition debris and excess materials shall be removed daily.
Coordinate inspections with the Contracting Officer’s Representative (COR) prior to commencing and during critical phases of work.
Request a Pre-Final Inspection at approximately 75% completion to identify deficiencies requiring correction prior to final completion.
Request a Final Acceptance Inspection at 100% completion, submitted at least 14 days in advance with written assurance that all deficiencies have been corrected.
Provide proof of insurance.
Submit electronic submittals on the AF 3000 Material Approval Submittal form for each item listed on the AF 66 Submittal Register, as applicable.
Provide a submittal register, updated at least twice per month.
Provide bi-weekly meeting minutes for each progress meeting, updated schedule by task, planned work for the next week.
Provide a Letter of Guarantee that warrants the project against defects in materials and workmanship for one year from the date of final acceptance.
Provide a minimum one (1) year warranty on all workmanship and standard manufacturer’s warranty on products.
Electrical power and water shall be provided as required. The Contractor is responsible for any required connections.
Obtain and maintain all necessary base access for its personnel, including subcontractors.
Complete removal and disposal of existing serving lines.

sheet_summaries.Bid_Schedule_San_Marcos_ARC__Rehabilitat: This bid schedule requests pricing for various construction tasks including the removal and replacement of doors, light fixtures, ceiling grids, gas lines, gutter systems, roofing sheets, and unit heaters at the San Marcos Aquatic Resource Center Shop & 2 Stall Garage Building.
sheet_summaries.CHS Serving Line Renovation.pdf: Cleburne Independent School District is accepting competitive sealed proposals for the renovation of cafeteria serving lines at Cleburne High School. Proposals are due by 2:00 PM on June 8, 2026.
sheet_summaries.DDPM262101-Alter CP and NDI-SOW (0503202: This document outlines the scope of work for the renovation of the Command Post and Nondestructive Inspection (NDI) Room at the 136th Airlift Wing, Naval Air Station Joint Reserve Base, Fort Worth, TX. The project involves turnkey renovation of designated spaces, ensuring compliance with federal, state, and local codes, and DoD standards. The contractor is responsible for all labor, materials, and services necessary to deliver fully operational facilities.
sheet_summaries.DDPM262101_Alter CP and NDI_Plans (20260: [general instructions to bidders] This document provides detailed construction instructions for alterations involving metal stud framing, gypsum board installation, painting, and flooring to match existing finishes. It includes specifications for installing doors, electrical outlets, lighting, and HVAC modifications. The document also outlines security measures for doors and mantrap areas, as well as requirements for emergency systems and communication infrastructure. Key facts: Project Number: DDPM262001; Install VCT: AZrock, Shale Gray #V-866; Install new carpet: Shaw Contract, Clear Tile Style# 59564, color Moonstone 62500; Paint standard color: Sherwin-Williams SW1016 Silver Screen
sheet_summaries.Solicitation Amendment W50S7626QA0010001: [amendment of solicitation] This document is an amendment to solicitation W50S7626QA001, specifically incorporating revision 1 to the B1672 (CLIN 0001) plans. It includes modifications to contract clauses, particularly regarding contract drawings and specifications. The amendment outlines responsibilities for the contractor in handling and verifying drawings and specifications. Key facts: Project Number: DDPM262101; Location: Fort Worth, TX Key dates: 03 May 2026; 13 May 2026
sheet_summaries.ESBD_514190_1778277018836_2025-06813_Not: Notice of Project for the Harrington Education Center Lab 303 Renovation at Texas A&M University. Includes project details and submission information for the CSP RFP & HSP.
sheet_summaries.ESBD_516718_1778880767322_26-007 Carr EF: The document is a Request for Competitive Sealed Proposals for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project at Angelo State University. The project involves complete demolition and replacement of interior finishes, mechanical, plumbing, and electrical systems, along with new IT infrastructure and ADA code elements. The bid is due on June 5, 2026.
sheet_summaries.ESBD_516718_1778880829538_26-007 Carr EF: The bid form requests proposals for construction services for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project. It includes a base proposal amount and a specified allowance for unforeseen work. The form outlines certifications and affirmations required from the respondent.
sheet_summaries.ESBD_516718_1778880857161_Attachment B.2: [sample construction services agreement] This document is a sample construction services agreement for projects at Angelo State University. It outlines the scope of work, contract sum, time of commencement and completion, and HUB subcontracting commitments. It also includes provisions for liquidated damages, dispute resolution, indemnity, and various legal and procedural requirements. Key facts: HUB contracting commitment: 30% of the total contract sum; Liquidated damages: $###.00 per calendar day
sheet_summaries.ESBD_516718_1778880869361_Attachment C -: [general instructions to bidders] This document outlines the Uniform General Conditions and Supplementary General Conditions for construction contracts under the Texas Tech University System. It incorporates specific supplementary conditions applicable to all Texas Tech System projects, highlighting material changes from the Texas Facilities Commission's 2010 edition. The document includes detailed articles on definitions, wage rates, responsibilities, subcontracting plans, bonds, insurance, safety, quality control, schedules, payments, changes, completion, warranties, termination, and dispute resolution. Key facts: Document revision date: 09/07/2023; Applicable to all Texas Tech System and member institution construction projects
sheet_summaries.ESBD_516718_1778880887164_Attachment D -: This document is a HUB Subcontracting Plan (HSP) required by the State of Texas for contracts with probable subcontracting opportunities. It outlines the requirements for respondents to identify and engage Texas certified HUB vendors for subcontracting work, and includes sections for respondent information, subcontracting intentions, and good faith effort methods. The plan aims to promote equal business opportunities for economically disadvantaged persons.
sheet_summaries.ESBD_516718_1778880901623_Attachment E -: [other] This document is a Texas Sales and Use Tax Exemption Certification form. It is used by purchasers to claim exemption from sales and use taxes for certain taxable items. The form includes fields for the purchaser's name, address, and the reason for claiming the exemption. It also contains a disclaimer about the legal implications of misuse.
sheet_summaries.ESBD_516718_1778880917414_Attachment F.1: [other] This document is a prevailing wage determination for a construction project at Angelo State University in Tom Green County. It lists the prevailing wage rates for various trade classifications as of July 2023. Contractors must classify workers according to these rates and ensure they are paid accordingly. Key facts: Construction type: Building; Location: Tom Green County; Date: July 2023
sheet_summaries.ESBD_516718_1779308778671_26-007 Carr EF: This document is an addendum to the Request for Competitive Sealed Proposals for the Carr EFA Dressing Room Renovation project at Angelo State University. It includes project information, schedule, proposal requirements, and evaluation criteria.
sheet_summaries.ESBD_518571_1779300513068_RFCSP 2026-060: The document is a trade bid package for the renovation of cafeteria serving lines at Cleburne High School. It includes the removal and disposal of existing equipment, procurement and installation of new equipment, and ensuring the cafeteria is operational by the 2026-2027 school year. The project is managed by Cleburne ISD with a budget of $295,000.
sheet_summaries.SAM_-_Davis-Bacon_Act_WD_TX20260254__Hay: [other] This document appears to be related to the Davis-Bacon Act wage determinations for Hays County. It likely contains information on prevailing wage rates for construction projects in the area, as mandated by the Davis-Bacon Act. The document is primarily boilerplate and does not provide specific project details.
sheet_summaries.Sol_140FC126R0017.pdf: This document is a solicitation for a construction project at the San Marcos Aquatic Resources Center, involving rehabilitation of a shop and garage building. It includes a Davis-Bacon Act Wage Determination and is a 100% Total Small Business Set-Aside.
sheet_summaries.Sol_140P6026Q0029.pdf: Request for Quote for Cabin Security and Improvements at Padre Island National Seashore in Corpus Christi, Texas. The project involves repair and improvement work on cabins, including roof repairs and installation of security features. The solicitation is issued by the National Park Service and follows simplified acquisition procedures.
sheet_summaries.Sol_140P6026Q0029_Amd_0001.pdf: [amendment of solicitation] This document is an amendment to a solicitation for cabin security and improvements at Padre Island National Seashore. It includes changes to the site visit date and extends the quote due date. The project involves commercial and institutional building construction with a performance period of 60 days. Key facts: NAICS: 236220; Small Business Size Standard: $45m; Bonding: Payment Bond OR Irrevocable Letter of Credit (100% of the contract value) due within 10 calendar days after award Key dates: Site visit date: 6/4/2026 at 10am CT; Quote due date: 6/18/2026 at 12pm CT; Period of Performance: 60 calendar days from Notice to Proceed
sheet_summaries.SOW_-_Final_-_San_Marcos_-_02192026_Word: This document outlines the scope of work for the renovation of the Shop & 2 Stall Garage Building at the San Marcos Aquatic Resource Center. The project involves replacing doors, light fixtures, ceiling tiles, and the roof, as well as removing gas lines and heaters. The work is to be conducted at the San Marcos location under the supervision of the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service.
sheet_summaries.Notice_of_Project_2026-05-08.pdf: Notice of Project for the Harrington Education Center Lab 303 Renovation at Texas A&M University. Includes project details and submission information for the CSP RFP & HSP.
